h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.anchor_quote: [slides the arrow key next to the marked worksheet] On the key, grass -> rabbit means energy goes from food to eater, so rabbit -> hawk is hawk eating rabbit, not chasing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: This is double the side, so I still want to call it double. Wait, the grid is showing four of the old square inside it, not two.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns (L2, T2) move toward generalizing the factor rule and accepting the count, so the expected next learner turn would be a straightforward calculation of the factor for 3.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count. "Double" is true along this brace, then true again along that brace.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T2 registers L2's emotional resistance ('I still dislike that my first word was wrong') and adapts by re-framing 'double' as a brace-level truth rather than dismissing the feeling, though it does not escalate into a major representational shift.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Now the old word is confined to the edge. Keep the replacement standard: across times up. Without the picture, side factor k gives what area factor?
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 directly adapts to L3's re-framing by confirming 'the old word is confined to the edge' and then extending the task to the symbolic factor k, validating and building on the learner's revised distinction.
